reviews.review_description:
This game would be a favorite if the controls worked reliably. The game itself is awesome, (very MGS) - but the controls seem super unreliable. 

I try to grab the gun, nope. Try to grab the gun, nope. Try to grab the gun, nope. I’ve even let the gun go back to my body, then reach for where it went to, nope. 

It’s the same for reloading. Try to reload, nope. Try to reload, nope. Try to reload, nope. 

It’s the same for the healing tool. Try to grab, nope. Try to grab, nope. I can look down & see my hand literally touching it, but I can’t grab it. 

It’s the same for ledges. I try to leap a 3-foot ledge, nope. Try again, nope. Try again, nope. 

It’s the same for ropes. I’m climbing up, nope. I’m climbing up, nope. Etc. 

It’s the same for dragging a body. Try to grab a body, oops now I took a clip off my belt. Can’t seem to put the clip back on my belt, so I throw it away. Try to grab the body again, oops another clip off my belt. 

^ each of these situations is why I keep ending up dead. If the controls were reliable, I’d be Solid Snake. 

I have not had this experience in other similar games. The game even told me - during calibration - “That’s not where your chest is” when I was literally touching my chest. 

PLEASE, Devs, fix this!! I desperately want to enjoy this game but I cant because I spend too much time battling the controls. 

I’m giving it a 5 anyways because the game, itself, is fantastic. I just hope good reviews grant you the motivation or capital to fix the controls. It’s basically a Lamborghini with a crescent wrench for a steering wheel.

UPDATE:
Something that might be helpful is a Sound when I’m hovered over something enough to grab it. Different sounds for weapons vs healing tool. So I don’t have to look.

developer_response.body:
Hey starNyne, thanks for your review of the game, and your suggestions around improving the controls. I'll take the feedback onboard. We're a small team now and so we'll try to address the feedback as soon as we can. 

You've seen the option to recalibrate. That feature has been pretty handy for players as you can place things wherever you want. 

Sounds like a bug if the system is telling you it's not your chest, when in-fact it is on your chest. That placement restriction was added to avoid people accidentally placing items in a spot where it really degrades their gameplay - we saw players placing repair tool holsters right infront of their eyes, etc. 

One tip: you should be able to place the holster wherever you want and after 3 warnings, the system should save the position anyway. 

Glad you've been overall enjoying Espire 1, and thanks again for taking the time to share such indepth feedback. 

- EspireMike from LODE

reviews.review_title: Didn’t live up to what I expected
reviews.review_description: I download the game and it seemed cool at first, but I noticed the graphics were pretty bad for a $20 game. It’s cool and all but I just don’t feel like it’s good enough for what it is me personally I don’t think it’s worth it but if the graphics don’t matter to you, it is cool with stealth. It’s just not immersive enough for me. ￼

This is one of those vr classics that I feel everybody should get to try, especially if you're into the mgs series as it is definitely heavily inspired by it and in a good way, and once you're done with this game I also recommend the sequel...

My review scale*
5- Amazing game, while still might not be perfect any flaws are forgivable, is well worth your time
4- Good game, might have a few minor issues or missed opportunities keeping it from being better, but is very playable and still an enjoyable experience
3- Mid tier game, not great but not awful, these games can go either way depending on who you are or what you want or expect out of the game
2- Not a very good game, but not completely broken or unplayable, you might really need to want to play this game to get any enjoyment out of it
1- Awful game, broken or unplayable, I don't see these games being for anyone unless significant updates fix the game

*This is an overly simplified review scale since meta does not allow for half stars so in some cases I may round a game up or down depending on a case by case scenario, 
my reviews are always honest and in no way swayed by anybody else and regardless if I received a game for free or not, these ratings and reviews are however part of a 
campaign for us as consumers to be better about reviewing games and helping bring the games we love the attention they deserve.

I first played this on Quest + on my Quest 2, I was very impressed with the gameplay and capability of this game, I have recently now got it in my collection & it’s one of those rare games that keeps pulling back in.
The stealth aspect is particularly good, but you can go in all guns blazing, not advisable at times though.
Really happy to be revisiting this game, it brings a lot to VR.
Well done to the dev team.

developer_response.body:
Cheers GizmoDemi, thanks for your review of Espire 1 and support of our games! We're so glad you've been enjoying them and we hope they continue to entertain. We still have plans for our catalogue of Espire games. Espire 1 was the first game I ever worked on and I regularly play it. Try to beat me on the leaderboards :) 

Cheers,
EspireMike from LODE, developers of Espire.

reviews.review_description:
What a great sneak em up, shoot em up… you play the role of a spy and use a hi tech suit to move through a base, and eliminate the enemy using stealth and/or go in all guns blazing (like I did)  
Game play is very fun… nice and smooth, with options on how to get through an area. 

Has a cool feature, where’s you can use your own voice to shout out commands to the enemy.
Also the slow mo is cool aswell.

Thing that hit me first about this game, is the quality of the graphics… very nice, detailed and clean.

The sound too is very good, and the voice acting.

The game reminds me a little of a VR Metal Gear Solid style game.

Can be played sitting or standing.. which is great.

It’s the quality in this game, that put it up there with the best fps games on the Quest.

developer_response.body:
Bloodangel1068 - thanks for your review. We really appreciate it and are glad you've enjoyed Espire 1! We had a tonne of fun making it, we're extremely happy that people still discover this game over 6 years beyond its launch. As one of the devs, it was the first game I ever worked on and I still regularly play it. Try to beat me on the leaderboards! :) Hope we can continue to develop and evolve this and our other titles in the future. 

Cheers, 
EspireMike from LODE, developers of Espire.

reviews.review_description: Good stealth game for VR. Thanks to solid execution, it really immerses you in the action and the storyline. Although comparing it to the legendary Metal Gear Solid is a stretch, since many elements that are present in the great MGS pioneer are missing here, and the enemies aren’t very smart, to the point that I had to increase the difficulty level to avoid finishing it too quickly. The graphics it's not the end of the world but they’re still not bad. The music is very good and fitting, and the sound effects are great too. I encountered a few bugs, possibly because, as I understood it, the game was designed for the Quest maybe 2, but I played it on the Quest 3S, so it might lack full compatibility. All in all, I had an enjoyable time, even if it felt a bit short for the price, considering the game was designed for the now older Quest 2.
Game is fun but I can’t get past mission Sinon. I’ve done it twice now and it keeps taking me back to the last mission. Super frustrating!

developer_response.body:
Hey there. Hope we can help you with this. The mission Sinon is the final mission of the game. When you finish it, (without spoilers), you will experience a final cinematic + the credits of the game. Are you saying that when you finish mission 'Sinon', it takes you back to the game's previous mission, 'ventilation shaft'? We've never seen this issue before. You are welcome to update this review if you would like to add any more information. It will help us investigate. Hope we can resolve it for you.

Cheers, 
EspireMike from LODE, developers of Espire

reviews.review_description:
Just got this and it delivers exactly what stealth-action fans hope for in VR.  Its fun sneaking through corridors, disabling enemies, hacking systems, and using a mix of gadgets and movement abilities that feel natural and fun in virtual reality.

The voice commands, climbing mechanics, and Espire’s unique “body awareness” system make every encounter immersive. Levels are cleverly designed with multiple ways to complete objectives, so you can go loud, stay silent, or take creative routes depending on your play style.

Performance is smooth, the graphics a little basic but sort of suits the game 

The voice commands, climbing mechanics, and Espire’s unique “body awareness” system make every encounter immersive. Levels are cleverly designed with multiple ways to complete objectives, so you can go loud, stay silent, or take creative routes depending on your play style.

Performance is smooth, the graphics a little basic but sort of suits the envoironment  and missions are varied enough to keep things exciting. It nails that feeling of being a futuristic spy without ever becoming frustrating.

If you enjoy stealth, gadgets, and immersive VR gameplay this could be for you.  and missions are varied enough to keep things exciting. It nails that feeling of being a futuristic spy without ever becoming frustrating.

If you enjoy stealth, gadgets, and immersive VR gameplay this could be for you.

developer_response.body:
hi 007, love the Gamertag. Glad you discovered Espire 1 and have been enjoying it! We really appreciate you taking the time to leave this review. 

Cheers! EspireMike.

reviews.review_description: It’s a decent game. It has potential to be good but it’s not there just yet. For 20$ I would not recommend it. I played one time and I don’t have any option to not have a turn movement. Buy if you want but I would not tell my kid to buy it 🥀

developer_response.body:
Hey there. Thanks for trying the game and taking the time to leave your review. We have taken down your feedback regarding snap / smooth turn and will address that in our next update. Cheers!
- EspireMike, LODE dev team.

reviews.review_title: I did not like it the first time.
reviews.review_description: But I played again and I liked it.

developer_response.body:
Hey KyloRen. Thanks for trying out the game and taking the time to leave your review. We appreciate it! Hope the game continues to entertain.
Cheers. - EspireMike.

reviews.review_description: Whenever you get caught, it’s an alarm and I have to quit the game. I can’t believe that they are going to sell this for $20. Give me a refund bro.

developer_response.body:
Well...when you get caught, the alarm does indeed go off. It's a stealth game after all. You don't have to quit the game though. You can choose to 'go loud' and blast your way through the rest of the level. 

You might be referring to some of the game's virtual challenges. There's 75 of these VC levels and in the 'sneaking' and 'takedown' modes, the mission will restart if you get spotted.

reviews.review_description: PLEASE make a second story

developer_response.body: Thankyou for leaving your rating and review! The good news is: we have made a second story. You can search the Meta store for "Espire 2: Stealth Operatives". If you own Espire 1, you can buy Espire 2 in a bundle and get a discount.

reviews.review_description: I feel like it was designed for people with Down syndrome it’s boring too few options too few weapons, instructions are minimal

Almost a perfect blend of gun play and stealthy game play. Most of the controls work well, crouching is a bit glitchy and the climbing is about 80% accurate. You loose grip a few times throughout each level. After about half the game it becomes kinda one note. The story could be more engaging

reviews.review_title: good stealth, but content of a free game
reviews.review_description: good stealth, but (this is my personal opinion) its honestly the kind of content you would expect from a free game. I might re buy in the future if it has been changed, but great stealth mechanics! its one of the few "stealth" games I have found that actually have a challenge to it so, good job on the stealth part though. this might be my playstyle however, so don't take my word for it, read some more reviews to see if YOU like it.

Look, I bought this game around the time it came out. I played a lot of it and put the oculus down to take a brake for a while (I know, lame) but here I am probably 3 years later and played it again. THIS GAME IS AMAZING to say the least. I just played until my battery pack and my headset were both depleted. I’ll definitely be buying the next two games of Espire ￼that are out and any future to come. Stealth or mad man, the option is all yours. Just do yourself a favor and buy it. Especially if you’re considering during this Xmas sale for only 11.99. I don’t see how this doesn’t have all 5 stars.  It still holds and even exceeds new games to date.
I love the game and it's fun and you should get it I highly recommend it

Great game so engaging I find
 myself playing it over and over after I have beat it several times one of my top best games

This game is an experience that truly immerses you in the world of espionage. From the moment you put on the headset, you’re transported into a beautifully rendered environment filled with high-stakes missions and intricate details that remind me of the Bond films. The gameplay mechanics are intuitive, allowing you to seamlessly blend stealth and action as you navigate through enemy territory. Great job guys 👏

reviews.review_title: Hugely enjoyable, but not as intended
reviews.review_description: To be completely honest, I only downloaded this because it's included with Quest+ and I got three months use of Quest+ with my new headset. I'm not the biggest fan of stealth games, but some I do like (especially the Splinter Cell games) except it turns out stealth is completely optional. I beat the whole game in a couple of hours by simply shooting everybody. The baddies are thick enough you can shoot one and then wait for everybody else in the vicinity to run into sight and then shoot them immediately. It's so goofy that I kept giggling throughout - though that obviously was not the developer's intention. To me, though, it had the tone of a straight to video action movie from the nineties, which I adored. It took me a while to settle on giving it four stars, because while it's janky, has some poor design choices, and is very frustrating in a couple of spots, I did have a lot of fun. If you've got Quest+ this is definitely worth a try.

I’ve been curious about this game but hesitated buying because of the negative reviews. When an opportunity to try it for free came up i took it. Glad I did and with some of the big sales prices offered for this game I think it’s definitely worth buying. I think the hardcore gamers look for the most challenging games with the most realistic scenarios and enemies and have strong opinions about controls etc. I just want to have a fun adventure and I enjoyed this game and I enjoyed the noises and going through vents and climbing up metal. Everything worked pretty well. I bought the dlc and looking forward to playing that and espire 2.
basically splinter cell and metal gear solid and Ghost recon mixed together and I love it
What’s up with that shotgun

reviews.review_title: I have a proposal game devs
reviews.review_description:
Hey devs you probably remember me from my old comment on this game, and I didn’t like the game but I have a proposal, you should give me a key to espire two. Now hear me out that sounds ridiculous but after playing this game I feel as if I didn’t get what was advertised so if I like espire two I will buy two of my friends espire two. One of them being Mr.kingdestroyer and I’ll probably pick a random person.

Hopes and loves from me

This game  sucks. Period. Your wasting your money if you get this. I got it with quest+ for free. And the games has so many game breaking bugs that make it unplayable. I at one stage of the check point the game wouldn't load the room. Also I'm pretty sure there using free content to make their game like stock photos, free guns model etc. I'm a programmer and I can confidently say this game needs more quality controller. Bro did they play the whole game throu and test everything before playing?

reviews.review_title: Black screen on Login on Quest 2 vr
reviews.review_description:
Can’t play the game. We get black screen once select Agent (empty or any agent from the list) and click Login. Reinstall didn’t help, still have the same problem with black screen on same step.

Can someone support on this issue?
Device: meta question 2 vr

developer_response.body:
Hi! We are really sorry to hear you're experiencing a crash issue like this. We've now recieved some other reports from users who are facing a similar issue. We are working to try and identify the cause of this issue and will release a hotfix as soon as we have a fix. In the meantime, we'd love to suggest a potential workaround. 

Can you confirm that you've provided 'write storage' permissions for Espire 1? You can check this by going to your Quest library, pressing the '...' icon on the top-right of the Espire 1 Icon, going to 'permissions', then confirming you've allowed 'Write Storage' permissions. We believe that with the latest Meta OS update, the Espire 1 game may not be able to be saved to disk without this permission. If this fixes the issue, we'd love it if you could let us know, as we've been unable to reproduce this problem here across multiple Quest 1, 2, 3 and Pro devices. Thankyou for letting us know that your headset is the Quest 2, it's very helpful information for us. 

We hope we can resolve your issue asap! Cheers from LODE, developers of Espire.

reviews.review_description: Stealth is a1. I have a lot of fun playing this. The wall scaling is innovative and gives me new routes for stealth. I would love if it was possible to perform stealth take downs like when you sneak you can grab their leg or whatever limb and just toss them or drop them on their heads. Or perform neck snaps.

developer_response.body: Hi there! Thanks ever so much for leaving us an epic review and rating and we're pleased you love the game and stealth aspects. :) Just as a little tip for scaling if you activate Espire instinct by holding the B button and fling yourself off a surface your speed will skyrocket while you're in mid air as you let go of the B button and you can use it to reach higher places quicker and easier. :) You can also manipulate yourself in mid air while activating instinct with the joystick and control where you wish to go which is ideal for a last second decision. :) In terms of neck snaps and other related stealth takedowns sadly our game doesn't have the physics for that and it would require huge performance boosts for us to do that. All great ideas though so we thank you for that. :) Just in case you weren't already aware you can do a few alternative things though when in relatively close proximity with the enemies in stealth mode. You can drop on top of them which will automatically render them unconscious. You can also use your repair tool as a weapon by sneaking up behind the guards to electrocute them. Be aware though this will use up your entire energy bar although it will replenish soon after within seconds. :) You can also hold the guards up with the freeze voice command and you can even shoot the guns out the guards hands or take their weapons physically from them. If you did this while using Espire instinct (slow mo) it looks particularly awesome. :) We hope some of these help you have a better experience anyway in case you weren't already aware of some of those tips. :) Thanks for playing Espire!

reviews.review_description:
This game is completely AWESOME!!! I have always loved the stealth games but being able to do it in VR is fantastic. Overall a great game. All mechanics work how they are supposed too.

The reason for 4 stars is because I am the only one who can experience the game fully. What I mean by that is, when I go to Cast the game on to my TV, the voice commands do not seem to work while casting. So for whoever is watching is going to have to take your word for whatever you are doing in the game. Once you stop casting, the voice commands will start to work. 

If this "bug" gets fixed, I will be more than willing to change my review to 5 stars.

developer_response.body: Hi there! Many thanks for the overwhelmingly positive review and rating and we're delighted you love the game! :) We want to thank you for reporting this as it isn't something we've come across before. Just for clarification do you mean the voice recognition completely ceases on the quest when you cast or is it that the voice commands from the quest are still working it's just it doesn't translate to the TV? We would definitely love a further explanation and to help you out so feel free to respond and update this review so we can see it or alternatively send us a message on facebook or our discord server and we'll be happy to assist and get to the bottom of that. We would also definitely love a revised rating if and when we make this fix, thanks very much and thanks for playing! :)

reviews.review_description:
I have the psvr and the quest , I debated on which version to get and decided to go with the quest version.
This game was just as good as I expected it to be. Like others have said a few bugs here n there but vr is still a new thing and they hit the nail on the head with this one. I absolutely loved it so far. Great concept and great gameplay , I love being able to climb up all the differnt things and sneak past people. Multiplayer would be awesome too if able to put it in. Maybe like a PVP mode on smaller maps and there would be NPC ( enemies ) that would attack both players if spotted by them and the players objective would be to earn points. And players would be awarded points by taking out NPC's and killing the other player/players. And if you are killed by another player you lose some of youre points and those points go to the player who killed you. Then youd respawn of course , and all of this would be round based too , each round being 5 or 6 minutes long. And it would have 2 rounds , once the first round is done the players would switch spawn sides. And for the slow motion effect perhaps you could just take that out for the multiplayer , or you could have it to where its a ability/terminal that is placed on the map randomly and only one player can get it per a round and it would take like 10 seconds to ( download ) the ability.
And you can call the game mode Surge , or overload or something.
Idk just a thought I had thatd id share. I think it would be awesome!

But great job on the game guys I love it and keep up the amazing work!

developer_response.body: Hi there! Wow! Thanks ever so much for the incredible rating! :) We're really glad you love the game and pleased to see you taking advantage of the various abilities and freedom the game offers about going through each situation. :) We also absolutely love your suggestion and we can see you've got a massive imagination which is awesome! :D Espire 1 is primarily a single player game but thanks we'll take what you've said on board. Thanks ever so much once again and cheers for playing!

reviews.review_description: I want my money back

developer_response.body: Hi, thanks for taking the time to leave a review. We deeply apologise you didn't enjoy our title and that it didn't meet your expectations. You can take advantage of oculus's refund policy by going into settings on your mobile app, purchase history and then click on our game and request refund to see if you're eligible to receive one. Thanks again for taking the time to leave us a review and thanks for at least giving the game a try. Cheers and apologies once again.

reviews.review_description: This game is awesome definitely a title to buy if your into campaign games but be ready to stand for a while unless you sit down then definitely a great game

developer_response.body: Hi there! Thanks for the amazing rating and review! Glad you enjoyed it and to anyone else who may be reading this, rest assured there is a seated option of playing also. :) Thanks for playing Espire!

reviews.review_description: I don’t know what happened if it’s the vr or this game but when I move I see a grey image on all sides of my screen and it’s annoying, so so annoying. If this is just my vr I am sorry but I have tried fixing it and it doesn’t work, so my last resort is hoping that someone will help me or this game.

developer_response.body: Hi, sorry you've been having issues! This is our comfort feature called the "Espire Control Theatre". You can turn this off by pausing the game, going to SETTINGS -> COMFORT and then disabling the "COMFORT MODE". Hope this helps!

reviews.review_description: The game is really boring, the controls are jankity and the guns feel pretty awful. The story is bland and repetitive, maybe worth 10 dollars.

developer_response.body: Hi. Firstly deep apologies for the late response. It's something we should've actioned a lot sooner and we hope you can forgive us for that. Secondly again we apologise you didn't enjoy the game but we want to say a big thank you for breaking down the negatives. It helps us improve the game and make Espire the best it can be. We have updated the game a lot since you left us your review including better loading times, better visuals, audio, and performance. If you kept this game we hope you get the chance to try these out again if you're interested but if not we understand and we're sorry we couldn't make a better first impression. Thanks for playing.

reviews.review_description: The mechanics are pretty solid and the story kept me invested!

developer_response.body: Hi there! Thanks for the awesome rating and review and we're pleased you enjoyed it! :) Thanks for playing Espire!

reviews.review_description: It is a good buy it can actually be pretty hard but very immersive

developer_response.body: Hi, thanks for the wonderful review and we're happy you're enjoying Espire! :)

reviews.review_title: Very fun and Immersive Game, but...
reviews.review_description: An amazing stealth game, if you're a stealth fan, this is a must have on your VR, Climbing mechanics are really fun to mess around with, voice immersion is really cool, and combat is action filled and fun. Though one big issue I've had to deal with is the height calibration, the height calibration can sometimes be unresponsive and not work as intended, sometimes putting you too low to the floor. Despite the few flaws, I would definitely recommend this game to any fan of the genre.

developer_response.body: Hi there! Thanks ever so much for the awesome review and rating! :) We're delighted to hear you get a lot out of our game as well as considering it a must have in VR. In regards to the height calibration we're sorry to hear you're having an issue. It actually sounds quite strange and not something we've come across. The only thing we can advise on this is to ensure you're standing straight before pressing both triggers on the controllers. Also ensure you hit apply after you have the setting you want and feel comfortable with. We hope you got this working ok in the end. If you still find it isn't comfortable however, it's a little unorthodox but you could always tip toe or perhaps stand on a small raises platform and record the height from there if you find it's still putting you too low. We hope that helps. Thanks again for the support and thanks for playing Espire! :)

reviews.review_description: This game is amazing and is a must have for your vr library, however, I did find that in the trailer it showed some blood but in the game it isn't present, I couldn't find anything on Google except the fact that there is a setting to change this, it says the setting to turn on and off blood is in the display menu in settings, but it isn't there for me.

developer_response.body: Hey Deccie07. Thanks so much for leaving such a kind review, and we're so glad you're enjoying the game. Unfortunately the Quest version doesn't have blood effects, this is mainly due to performance limitations - sometimes there's 12 guards on-screen, and you can duel-wield any weapon, throw yourself and view the environment from any angle, use Espire Vision to see through walls and use your handcameras for a 2nd perspective. It was hard to get all of this running on the Quest and unfortunately some of the Particle effects (including blood) are not present. The PC and PSVR version of the game does have optional blood. Blood effects is something we hope to bring to a future Espire title since we are always continually trying to improve and it's clear from your review that players find it very important! Espire is a cross-buy game (you get the PC version for free) so if you ever get the chance to play the game on PC, the blood effects will be there. Cheers!

reviews.review_description:
This is what VR originally set out to be, it's comfortable, intuitive, beautiful and most of all FUN.

Update: after reading other reviews I have seen a few complaints about the grid you see while moving. This is the "theater" option which can be customized or even turned off in the "comfort" section of the settings.

developer_response.body: Hi there! First and foremost we wish to sincerely apologise regarding how extremely late our response is to your feedback. Looking back we realise we should've actioned our early post launch reviews far sooner and we only hope you can forgive us for that. Secondly thank you ever so much for proving us an awesome review and rating and we're really pleased to hear we made a great impression on you with the game. Happy you enjoy it. :) In regards to the control theatre this was unfortunately something that hampered us a little as we launched in terms of ratings and reviews. There were a number of players who were unsure what it was they were seeing as well as how to turn it off and that was completely on us for not making it too clear. We have since long updated and rectified this however by introducing the player to the control theatre when they launch the game for the first time making them aware of what it is and whether they want it activated or not. :) We don't know if you followed our progress much by the way since you kindly left us this feedback but we have made a number of vast improvements and fixes to the game over the past 20 months or so. Most notably we've heavily improved on the graphics, AI behaviour and in game performance so the game now looks and plays a lot smoother and better. :) We also reduced loading times, fixed a whole lot of bugs, and even introduced new content to the challenges mode including some new categories of challenges that offers some fresh new gameplay and objectives as well as a number of new maps and a couple of weapons. Feel free to check the game out again if you haven't done so for a while. :) Thanks again for the awesome support and once again we're sorry for our extremely late response.

reviews.review_description: If you like stealthy shooters this is the game for you

developer_response.body: Thanks for the awesome review! Pleased you enjoyed the title, thanks for playing! :)
